results.rendered_html:
putrakā, -putrikā
1 A daughter.
2 A doll, puppet.
3 A daughter appointed to raise male issue for a father who has no sons; aputra'nena vidhinā sutāṃ kurvīta putrikāṃ . yadapatyaṃ bhavedasyāṃ tanmama syātsvadhākaram ...
4 The cotton or down of the tamarisk.
5
(pb) (At the end of comp.) Anything little or small of its kind; as in asiputrikā, khaṅgaputrikā &c.
Comp.
— putraḥ, -sutaḥ
1 a daughter's son who by agreement becomes the son of her father; see  Ms. 9. 127. 
2 a daughter who, being regarded as a son, returns to her father's house; (putrikaiva putraḥ; athavā putrikaiva sutaḥ putrikāsutaḥ so'pyaurasasama eva  Mit.  on  Y. 2. 128 ).
3 a grandson
— prasūḥ a mother of daughters.
— bhartṛ m. ‘a daughter's husband,’ a son-in-law.
